yacht.text: From Dutch 'jacht' (a hunt) — a fast ship; the spelling stayed Dutch.

rhythm.text: From Greek rhythmos — flow; famously written with no standard vowel.

silhouette.text: Named after Étienne de Silhouette, an 18th-century French minister.

schadenfreude.text: Borrowed whole from German: Schaden (harm) + Freude (joy).

onomatopoeia.text: Greek 'name-making' — words that sound like what they mean.

quinoa.text: From Quechua 'kinwa' — the Andean grain; spelling kept from the source.

mnemonic.text: From Mnemosyne, Greek goddess of memory — hence the silent m.

hierarchy.text: Greek hieros (sacred) + arkhia (rule) — a sacred order.
